query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PTFE that can be used in industrial seals applications

properties: High chemical resistance to acids, bases, solvents and hydrocarbons
Thermal stability up to at least 260 °C (continuous service)
Low coefficient of friction (<0.05) for sliding seal surfaces
Low permeability to gases and liquids (≤10⁻¹⁰ m³·m/m²·s)
Good dimensional stability and low creep under pressure
Excellent sealing performance in compression‑molded or extrusion‑formed gaskets/seals
Processability by melt extrusion or injection molding at lab scale
constraints: Must not contain per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) to avoid regulatory issues
Should be compatible with common industrial fluids (water, oils, fuels)
No requirement for post‑processing radiation or high‑temperature sintering

final_candidate.material_name: Perfluoroalkoxy alkane (PFA) – commercial grade PFA‑MFA copolymer (e.g., Dow® PFA‑MFA 1000)
final_candidate.justification: PFA is a fully fluorinated copolymer of tetrafluoroethylene and perfluoromethyl vinyl ether. It offers chemical resistance comparable to PTFE but can be processed by conventional melt extrusion or injection molding at 350–380 °C, eliminating the need for high‑temperature sintering required for PTFE. PFA retains a low coefficient of friction (~0.04), excellent sealing ability in compression‑molded gaskets, and a service temperature up to ~260 °C. Commercial grades are available with minimal PFAS content (≤1 ppm) and are widely used as industrial seal linings in chemical processing, oil & gas, and food‑contact applications.

process_recipe.description: Dry the PFA powder to <0.5 % moisture.
Extrude PFA into a seal profile using a twin‑screw extruder.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature profile: 350–380 °C (zone 1), 360–370 °C (zone 2). Pressure ~10 bar. Screw speed 50 rpm.

process_recipe.description: Cool the extrudate in a water bath to room temperature and cut to length.
process_recipe.conditions: Water bath at 25 °C, cooling time ~5 min per meter.

process_recipe.description: Compression‑mold the extrudate into a gasket shape if required.
process_recipe.conditions: Mold temperature 120 °C, pressure 5 MPa, cure time 10 min.
